title: OSS Compliance — per-finding fixes, table+modal UX, no branch creation

steps.description: Phase A migration: drop prepare/publish enum values + columns + delete historical rows + add auto_apply_safe to OssFinding + ORM updates + schema docs
Review S01 — schema additions, migration shape, pre-delete order, enum recreate pattern, ORM consistency
Phase A code removal: rip _run_worktree/WORKTREE_KINDS/discard_job/branch-delete from oss_service; remove prepare/publish from CLI + scanner mode; rewrite SKILL.md scan-only + per-finding fix
Review S03 — completeness of removal, no orphan refs, _run_fix placeholder shape, scanner mode defense, expected test failures listed

Phase B catalog: oss_check_catalog.py loader (Pydantic + cache + debug hot-reload); enumerate IDs (scan + AST-walk); populate oss_check_catalog.yaml via online research; auto_apply_safe flag on every Finding constructor; persistence wiring
Review S05 — catalog completeness, brand voice, schema validation, references quality, auto_apply_safe assignments, no silent fallbacks

Phase C CLI: iw oss fix <CHECK_ID> [--apply] + idempotent fix-recipe registry under orch/oss/fix_recipes/; replace dashboard _run_fix placeholder with real subprocess invocation; add run_fixes orchestrator for apply-all-safe
Review S07 — working-tree-only invariant, idempotency, recipe-vs-flag consistency, CLI shape, no git mutation calls
Phase D/E APIs: per-check fix/recheck/accept routes, apply-all-safe preview + apply routes, .iw/oss-accepted.yaml reader/writer service, SSE row-level events
Review S09 — endpoint surface vs design, working-tree-only invariant, Pydantic models, idempotency, SSE event shape, defensive validation

Phase D dashboard: replace oss.html with domain-grouped collapsible table; new finding modal fragment; filter chips with default = failing/human-required; SSE row-level updates replacing reload; pass catalog + accepted to template
Review S11 — layout vs design, modal accessibility (focus trap, ESC), Tailwind/JS lint, SSE consumer correctness, catalog wiring, no regressions
Phase E UI: per-row Re-run icon, Mark-accepted reason form (inline), Apply-all-safe preview dialog with deselectable per-file checkboxes
Review S13 — re-run flow, accept reason validation, apply-all-safe modal correctness, modal accessibility, server-side defensiveness re-check
Phase E CI integration: update .github/workflows/compliance-scan.yml to read .iw/oss-accepted.yaml and downgrade matched MUST findings in SARIF; new honor_accepted.py script; reference doc
Review S15 — hash consistency between dashboard and CI, workflow correctness, edge cases, removed unconditional fail, doc

Tests — catalog completeness (AST), accept-yaml round-trip + idempotency, fix-recipe idempotency contract, dashboard routes for new endpoints + removed routes 404, migration tests for hard cleanup, SSE row-update events, plus updates to existing OSS tests
Review S17 — AC coverage map, test isolation, idempotency test correctness, hash consistency golden test, migration test correctness, conventions

Phase F cleanup: delete dead templates/skill files; prune docs; one-time housekeeping for stale .git/worktrees/oss-* and refs/heads/iw-oss-publish; rewrite skill references/ (drop history_rewrite/fix_recipes/modes prepare-publish sections)
Review S19 — no residual references to old flow anywhere outside ai-dev/active/CR-00022/; worktree+branch cleanup verified; skill+docs consistent; no accidental deletions

Global cross-layer review — AC mapping, cross-layer integration, working-tree-only invariant end-to-end, brand voice consistency, no orphan refs, idempotency end-to-end, CI honor flow, migration safety, docs
QV: Linting (ruff + node --check on dashboard JS)
QV: Formatting
QV: Type checking (mypy)
QV: Unit tests
QV: Integration tests (real docker — testcontainers)
QV: Browser verification — verify scan + table + modal + per-finding fix + accept-risk + apply-all-safe + SSE in isolated worktree stack
